long_description: The display processor computer fails in flight, causing loss or corruption of head-down display pages and caution formatting.
pilot_actions: Transfer attention to standby instruments and remaining displays, declutter nonessential pages, and terminate tactical tasking if situational awareness is degraded.

long_description: The display processor loses HUD formatting or symbology generation in flight, degrading head-up attitude, aiming, and flight reference presentation.
pilot_actions: Revert to head-down or standby references, avoid demanding low-visibility or weapons-delivery tasks, and recover if pilot scan becomes overloaded.

long_description: Display pages freeze in flight and stop updating despite remaining visible, causing stale aircraft, sensor, or mission information.
pilot_actions: Treat frozen pages as invalid, switch to alternate pages or displays, and simplify the mission until valid data presentation is confirmed.

long_description: A display data-bus fault interrupts information flow to one or more screens in flight, causing blanking, partial symbology, or intermittent updates.
pilot_actions: Concentrate on remaining reliable displays, avoid heavy mode switching, and recover if core flight or tactical data cannot be monitored safely.

long_description: Caution or advisory formatting is degraded in flight, increasing the chance of missed alerts or poor fault prioritization.
pilot_actions: Increase instrument scan discipline, verify system status on alternate pages if available, and terminate nonessential tasking if alert clarity is poor.
